hypothesis: The first universe expansion screen may understate production behavior because new ETF candidates lacked sector mapping. Testing the same additions with production-like sector tags should show whether mapped ETF/oil candidates improve alpha.

parameters.single_causal_variable: candidate universe membership with sector map

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected because no mapped universe expansion improved EV in a majority of fixed windows. The best EV-sum variant still lost PnL, regressed two windows, added seven trades, and lowered win rate by up to 8.9 pp.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains sample-limited, so this tested deterministic mapped universe expansion instead.

rejection_reason: Production-like sector mapping makes the ETF/oil expansion worse, not better; the added trades dilute accepted A+B capital allocation.
next_retry_requires: Do not add XLE/USO or broad sector ETFs to the production watchlist based on current snapshots.
Do not retry mapped sector ETF expansion without a new event/news or regime-routing discriminator.
Future universe expansion should focus on individual names with stable multi-window candidate replacement evidence, not broad ETF baskets.
